meaning: n.[军]外垒,在屋外的作业或工作vt.在工作上胜过
examples: First, you have to outwork everyone else.
Individuals with this style tend to be very broad in their outwork and look at many alternatives. They focus on the long run.
He was a good workman himself, quick and deft, and it had always been a point of pride with him that no man should do any of his work for him or outwork him.
Kathleen told me, I have a motto: Every day, nobody will outwork me.
On the contrary, the limited number of positions in the Major Leagues motivates them to outwork their competitors.
